summary: Verified the new per-module CRUD permissions (modulePerms) feature end-to-end. Backend POST/PUT/GET round-trip works, login response includes modulePerms in roleDetails, legacy roles return empty {} (no DB migration needed). Frontend Create Role dialog renders module toggles + colored C/E/D buttons + +C/E/D all shortcuts; auto-migration on edit works (teacher canEdit:false -> empty, admin_role canEdit:true+canDelete:true -> all CRUD pre-filled). Fallback gating on /homework verified: teacher (canEdit:false) sees view-only, admin (canEdit:true fallback) sees Assign button. 6/6 backend tests + 9/9 frontend checks PASS. One stale credentials note: teach1 password is '12345678', not 'pass123' as recorded in /app/memory/test_credentials.md.

verified_checks.frontend_apply_all_shortcuts_visible_aft: PASS (+C all/+E all/+D all - conditional render gated on form.modules.length > 0)
verified_checks.frontend_create_homework_only_role_with_: PASS - GET returned modulePerms={'homework':{create:true,edit:true,delete:false}}, canEdit=false (global fallback OFF)
verified_checks.frontend_teacher_edit_no_premigration: PASS (all 6 module rows have C/E/D off)
verified_checks.frontend_homework_gating_teacher_view_on: PASS (no Assign Homework btn, no delete trash icons)
verified_checks.frontend_homework_gating_admin_canEdit_f: PASS (Assign Homework btn visible for admin_role even with empty modulePerms)

action_items: Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md: teach1 password is '12345678' not 'pass123' (returns 401 on /api/auth/login with pass123). Either update the doc OR reset the seeded password to pass123. Backend test test_teacher_login_includes_empty_module_perms already handles both values via fallback retry, so non-blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: Roles.js line 154 conditionally renders the +C/+E/+D all shortcuts only when form.modules.length > 0. This is correct UX but worth flagging to the spec verifier — the testids listed in the request (apply-create-all etc.) will not exist in the DOM until at least one module is toggled on.

Roles.js line 47-58 auto-migration only seeds CRUD=true when legacy role has canEdit=true. The delete sub-key also picks up role.canDelete fallback (verified: admin_role has canDelete:true so all D=true). Good.

AuthContext canEdit/canCreate/canDelete fallback chain works correctly: modulePerms[module][action] -> global canEdit/canDelete. Verified teacher (canEdit:false, empty modulePerms) hides Assign btn; admin_role (canEdit:true, empty modulePerms) shows it.
Backend backfill in routers/auth.py lines 48-72 returns modulePerms={} for legacy roles in both GET /api/roles AND POST /api/auth/login -- confirmed via tests.
test_credentials.md is stale (teach1 password mismatch) — minor doc issue.
test_credentials: admin/12345678 (works) and teach1/12345678 (works) — teach1/pass123 from test_credentials.md returns 401
seed_data_creation: Created _test_homework_only role via UI; deleted immediately after verification via DELETE /api/roles/{id} (status 200). No residual seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: modulePerms backend + frontend feature fully verified. Per-module CRUD UI is gated behind toggling a module on. Fallback to global canEdit/canDelete still works for legacy seeded roles (teacher, admin_role, office_staff). teach1 actual seeded password is 12345678, update /app/memory/test_credentials.md accordingly when convenient. Use the backend test pattern in test_module_perms.py as regression suite for future role/permission changes.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Implementation matches spec; auto-migration on edit, conditional shortcut rendering, and fallback gating are all working.
